# Agent Audit

> Lighthouse-powered MCP server that turns performance, accessibility, SEO, and LLM-visibility audits into structured fix packs for AI coding agents.

Agent Audit is an open-source MCP server published as `@fullstackdegen/agent-audit`. It runs Google Lighthouse against a target URL and converts the output into structured fix packs that AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Codex) can execute autonomously.

## What it does

- Runs mobile and desktop Lighthouse audits (fast: 1 run, reliable: 3-run medians)
- Adds same-page intelligence: broken links, canonical tags, robots signals, JSON-LD, Open Graph, LLM visibility, llms.txt readiness
- Merges findings into a prioritized issue list
- Generates `fixPacks` with repo search hints, implementation steps, and measurable acceptance criteria
- Returns strict MCP `structuredContent` plus equivalent Markdown

## Install

```bash
npx -y @fullstackdegen/agent-audit
```

## MCP tool

`analyze_website_performance` — accepts a URL and optional mode (`fast` or `reliable`).
